ddls:
CREATE TABLE "attractions" (
  "attraction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each attraction */,
  "attraction_name" TEXT /* Name of the attraction */,
  "thrill_level" TEXT /* Level of thrill associated with the attraction */,
  "suitable_age_groups" TEXT /* Age groups suitable for the attraction */,
  "ride_type" TEXT /* Type of ride (e.g., flat, dark ride) */,
  "scenery_detail" TEXT /* Details about the scenery associated with the attraction */,
  "capacity" INTEGER /* Maximum number of visitors the attraction can handle at a time */,
  "opening_date" TEXT /* Date when the attraction opened */,
  "closing_date" TEXT /* Date when the attraction is scheduled to close */,
  "location" TEXT /* Physical location within the park */,
  "operator" TEXT /* Name of the operator or company managing the attraction */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current operational status (e.g., Open, Closed, Under Maintenance) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("attraction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_attractions_ride_type FOREIGN KEY ("ride_type") REFERENCES ride_types ("ride_type_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_attractions_thrill_level FOREIGN KEY ("thrill_level") REFERENCES thrill_levels ("thrill_level_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_attractions_suitable_age_groups FOREIGN KEY ("suitable_age_groups") REFERENCES age_groups ("age_group_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "age_groups" (
  "age_group_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each age group */,
  "age_group_name" TEXT /* Name of the age group (e.g., Young Children, Adults) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the age group's characteristics */,
  "min_age" INTEGER /* Minimum age for the age group */,
  "max_age" INTEGER /* Maximum age for the age group */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("age_group_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "thrill_levels" (
  "thrill_level_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each thrill level */,
  "thrill_level_name" TEXT /* Name of the thrill level (e.g., Low, Moderate, High) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the thrill level */,
  "safety_rating" INTEGER /* Safety rating of the thrill level (1-5) */,
  "recommended_height" INTEGER /* Recommended minimum height for the thrill level */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("thrill_level_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ride_types" (
  "ride_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each ride type */,
  "ride_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the ride type (e.g., Flat, Dark) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the ride type */,
  "average_duration" INTEGER /* Average duration of the ride in minutes */,
  "max_capacity" INTEGER /* Maximum number of visitors the ride can handle per session */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("ride_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "visitors" (
  "visitor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each visitor */,
  "visitor_name" TEXT /* Full name of the visitor */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age of the visitor */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the visitor for feedback */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the visitor for contact */,
  "member_status" TEXT /* Whether the visitor is a member (e.g., Yes, No) */,
  "join_date" TEXT /* Date when the visitor joined as a member */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("visitor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ratings" (
  "rating_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each rating entry */,
  "attraction_id" INTEGER /* ID of the attraction being rated */,
  "visitor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the visitor providing the rating */,
  "rating" INTEGER /* Rating score given by the visitor (1-5) */,
  "review" TEXT /* Review text provided by the visitor */,
  "review_date" TEXT /* Date when the review was submitted */,
  "revisit_intent" TEXT /* Visitor's intent to revisit the attraction (e.g., Yes, No) */,
  "visitor_age_group" TEXT /* Age group of the visitor at the time of the review */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rating_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ratings_visitor_id FOREIGN KEY ("visitor_id") REFERENCES visitors ("visitor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ratings_attraction_id FOREIGN KEY ("attraction_id") REFERENCES attractions ("attraction_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "maintenance" (
  "maintenance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each maintenance record */,
  "attraction_id" INTEGER /* ID of the attraction that underwent maintenance */,
  "maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date when maintenance was performed */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the maintenance performed */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the maintenance (e.g., Completed, Pending) */,
  "next_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the next scheduled maintenance */,
  "maintenance_cost" INTEGER /* Cost of the maintenance performed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("maintenance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_maintenance_attraction_id FOREIGN KEY ("attraction_id") REFERENCES attractions ("attraction_id")
)
